title: mentha arvensis var. scribae
intro: Mentha arvensis var. scribae, also known as American wild mint, is a variety of the peppermint plant that grows in wetland areas and is native to North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. The health benefits of American wild mint include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce nausea and vomiting, and even help to manage stress and anxiety. American wild mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
